steps.title: Identify the belief being overturned
steps.content: The passage opens with tool use in general, then pivots: “For a long time, people thought tool use was unique to primates.” The shift with “though” and “busted the myth” signals this old belief is being proven wrong. The examples—sea otters using anvil stones, woodpecker finches using cactus spines—establish that tool use isn't limited to primates.

steps.title: Match the overturning to the choices
steps.content: Choice C directly restates this reversal: long-held beliefs were wrong; primates aren't the only tool users. A contradicts the passage (animals find it easy, not difficult), B narrows focus to a comparison between sea otters and primates rather than addressing the exclusivity claim, and D dilutes one specific myth into vague “many myths.” This matches choice C — C is correct.
